metadata.archetype_justification: DVC is a pure CLI tool and Python library distributed via PyPI. It has no persistent data store of its own, no deployable service endpoints, no user-specific state, and no write operations to external services during normal operation. All operations are local filesystem and git operations.
metadata.context: Data Version Control: git-for-ML-data, models, and experiments.

top_gaps.title: Monolith vs Microservices
top_gaps.gap: Single tightly-coupled monolithic CLI application with no module boundaries or independent deployability
top_gaps.impact: Limits independent scaling; prevents cloud-native distribution

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute — no IaC, no ECS/EKS/Lambda definitions
top_gaps.impact: No cloud deployment model exists; entirely client-side execution

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No VPC, subnets, or security groups — no deployed infrastructure
top_gaps.impact: No network security posture to evaluate

top_gaps.title: Business Metrics
top_gaps.gap: No custom business metrics published to any monitoring system
top_gaps.impact: No visibility into usage patterns or operational health

top_gaps.title: Incident Response Automation
top_gaps.gap: No runbooks, no incident response automation
top_gaps.impact: Ad hoc response to production issues

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists in the repository (README.rst, extensive docs referenced at dvc.org/doc)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A RAG-based knowledge agent that indexes DVC documentation and source code to answer developer questions
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Generate embeddings from README.rst and inline documentation; connect to dvc.org/doc content

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 3) — GitHub Actions workflows for tests, builds, and security scanning
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that triggers deployments, checks build status across the test matrix, and manages release workflows
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: GitHub Actions API integration needed for agent-triggered operations

decomposition_strategy.rationale: Extract server-side capabilities (experiment queue, Studio API) while maintaining the CLI monolith for local operations

findings.title: No managed compute infrastructure
findings.description: No managed compute infrastructure exists. DVC is distributed exclusively as a PyPI package and executes entirely on the user's local machine. No ECS/EKS/Lambda/Fargate/EC2 definitions found.
findings.gap: No cloud compute model exists. All processing is client-side.
findings.recommendation: If cloud-hosted DVC capabilities are desired, define compute infrastructure using EKS (preferred) with Terraform or CDK. The existing Celery-based experiment queue worker is a natural candidate for cloud-hosted compute.

findings.title: No network security infrastructure
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network configuration exists. DVC has no deployed infrastructure.
findings.gap: No network security posture exists because there is no deployed infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: If cloud infrastructure is introduced, define VPC with private subnets, least-privilege security groups, and VPC endpoints for AWS service access.

findings.title: No API entry point
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, CloudFront, or any managed entry point exists. DVC exposes a Python API and CLI consumed locally only.
findings.gap: No managed API entry point because there is no deployed service.
findings.recommendation: If a cloud-hosted DVC API is created, use API Gateway with throttling, authentication, and request validation.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration exists. There is no deployed compute, database, or managed service that could be scaled.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling because there is no deployed infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: If cloud compute is introduced, configure auto-scaling with appropriate min/max for EKS node groups or ECS services.

findings.title: Partial IaC coverage
findings.description: CI/CD infrastructure is partially defined as code via GitHub Actions workflow YAML files. However, no infrastructure provisioning IaC exists (no Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK).
findings.gap: No IaC for infrastructure provisioning. Only CI/CD pipeline definitions exist as code.
findings.recommendation: Define all infrastructure in IaC. Use Terraform or CDK for any future cloud infrastructure from the start.

findings.title: CI/CD automation with minor gaps
findings.description: Comprehensive CI/CD via GitHub Actions covering tests, build, publish, security scanning, plugin tests, and benchmarks. Missing automated rollback for PyPI publishes.
findings.gap: No automated rollback mechanism. Once published to PyPI, a version cannot be easily unpublished.
findings.recommendation: Add automated validation gates between Test PyPI and Production PyPI publish. Add smoke tests from Test PyPI before production publish.

findings.title: Monolithic application with tight coupling
findings.description: DVC is a single deployable monolithic Python package with 298 source files. All functionality lives in a single artifact with tight internal coupling.
findings.gap: Single tightly-coupled monolith with no independently deployable services. All components must be released together.
findings.recommendation: Apply Strangler Fig pattern to extract server-side capabilities: extract experiment queue worker as independent EKS service, create cloud-hosted API for experiment tracking.

findings.title: Limited API versioning strategy
findings.description: DVC exposes a Python API with a public surface area but no formal versioning strategy beyond package-level semver.
findings.gap: No explicit API versioning strategy beyond package-level semver. No per-function deprecation policy.
findings.recommendation: Implement explicit deprecation decorators for Python API functions. Document API stability guarantees. Use URL path versioning for any future REST API.

findings.title: Environment variable-based service discovery
findings.description: DVC uses environment variables for service endpoint configuration and Python entry points for plugin discovery. Appropriate for CLI but limits flexibility.
findings.gap: Service endpoints configured via environment variables rather than dynamic discovery.
findings.recommendation: Current approach is appropriate for CLI architecture. Adopt AWS Service Discovery if cloud-hosted services are introduced.

findings.title: No managed object storage provisioned
findings.description: DVC tracks unstructured data but stores it on user-configured remote backends. DVC itself does not provision S3 buckets or parsing pipelines.
findings.gap: No managed storage layer with parsing capabilities for operational data.
findings.recommendation: If cloud-hosted features are introduced, use S3 for unstructured data storage with Athena for experiment result analysis.

findings.title: Mostly centralized data access layer
findings.description: DVC has a well-structured data access layer through fsspec filesystem abstraction providing unified interface to multiple storage backends.
findings.gap: Some auxiliary code paths access the filesystem directly without the unified layer.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining fsspec-based unified data access. Ensure all new data access uses the centralized abstraction.

findings.title: No audit logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail, audit logging, or immutable log storage is configured. DVC has no deployed infrastructure that would produce CloudTrail events.
findings.gap: No audit logging infrastructure. No CloudTrail, no immutable log storage.
findings.recommendation: If cloud infrastructure is introduced, enable CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 Object Lock for immutable storage.

findings.title: Environment variable-based secrets without rotation
findings.description: DVC uses environment variables for all secret values. No plaintext credentials committed. CI uses GitHub Secrets. No dedicated secrets management with rotation.
findings.gap: No dedicated secrets management service with automated rotation. Credentials managed via environment variables.
findings.recommendation: For a CLI tool, environment variable-based secrets are appropriate. Adopt AWS Secrets Manager with rotation if cloud services are introduced.

findings.title: Limited compute hardening
findings.description: No deployed compute to harden. CI/CD includes CodeQL scanning and Dependabot. Ruff with Bandit rules enabled. No runtime vulnerability scanning.
findings.gap: Security scanning limited to source code. No runtime vulnerability scanning or container scanning.
findings.recommendation: Add pip-audit to CI for Python-specific vulnerability detection. If containers are introduced, add container image scanning.

findings.title: Security scanning without blocking gate
findings.description: CodeQL SAST runs on every push/PR with security-extended queries. Dependabot configured for daily dependency scanning. No blocking security gate.
findings.gap: CodeQL may not block on critical findings. No explicit security gate preventing merge of vulnerable code.
findings.recommendation: Add blocking security gate to CI pipeline. Add pip-audit for real-time dependency vulnerability checking.

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or distributed tracing instrumented. DVC uses standard Python logging only.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing. DVC could instrument tracing for dependent applications but does not.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry SDK as optional dependency to instrument DVC operations for trace propagation through consuming applications.

findings.title: No business metrics
findings.description: No custom business metrics published to any monitoring system. Anonymous telemetry exists but is not accessible as operational metrics.
findings.gap: No business metrics published to monitoring. Usage telemetry exists but not as operational metrics with dashboards.
findings.recommendation: Publish custom metrics (command execution counts, error rates, data transfer sizes) to CloudWatch or metrics backend.

findings.title: No anomaly detection or alerting
findings.description: No anomaly detection or alerting configured. Only CI failure Slack notifications exist.
findings.gap: No anomaly detection, no alerting on error rates or latency.
findings.recommendation: Monitor PyPI download trends, GitHub issue rates, and CI pipeline health. Implement CloudWatch anomaly detection if cloud services are introduced.

findings.title: Basic two-stage deployment without canary
findings.description: Two-stage PyPI publishing (Test PyPI on main push, Production PyPI on release). No canary or gradual rollout.
findings.gap: No canary or staged rollout to end users. Binary publish model.
findings.recommendation: Implement staged release strategy: release candidates, GitHub pre-releases, post-publish smoke tests.

findings.title: Integration tests with coverage gaps
findings.description: Integration tests exist across multiple tiers (7 integration, 104 functional, plugin tests). Coverage could be expanded for cloud backends.
findings.gap: Limited integration test coverage for cloud storage backends beyond dvc-s3. No DVC Studio contract tests.
findings.recommendation: Expand integration tests for DVC Studio API contract, multi-backend tests, end-to-end experiment workflows.

findings.title: No incident response automation
findings.description: No runbooks, no incident response automation, no self-healing patterns. Only Slack notification on CI failure.
findings.gap: No incident response automation. All response is ad hoc.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for PyPI publish failures, CI failures, security vulnerability responses. Automate with GitHub Actions.

findings.title: No observability ownership
findings.description: No observability ownership defined. No CODEOWNERS for monitoring, no dashboards, no named alarm owners.
findings.gap: No observability ownership. No dashboards, no alarms with owners.
findings.recommendation: Define observability ownership. Create CODEOWNERS for workflows. Establish dashboard ownership for any future cloud services.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No AWS resource tagging because no AWS resources are provisioned.
findings.gap: No resource tagging governance because no cloud resources exist.
findings.recommendation: If AWS resources are introduced, implement tagging governance from the start with mandatory tags and enforcement.

triggering_questions.note: Monolithic CLI application with no service boundaries

immediate_actions: Extract experiment queue as EKS service

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 but contextual guard blocks: DVC is a CLI tool/library distributed via PyPI, not a VM-hosted service. No EC2 workloads to containerize.

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold but guard prevents trigger — no EC2/VM workloads exist to containerize

pathways.not_triggered_reason: DVC is a PyPI-distributed CLI tool, not a VM-hosted service. There are no EC2 workloads to containerize. The pathway contextual guard (SHALL NOT trigger if compute is not EC2/VM-based) prevents triggering.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures); no commercial database engines detected

triggering_questions.note: Score 4 — no stored procedures, all logic in application layer. Threshold not met.

pathways.not_triggered_reason: DATA-Q4=4 does not meet the <3 threshold. No commercial database engines detected in the repository.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2 Not Evaluated (no persistent data store surface); no self-managed databases detected

triggering_questions.note: Not Evaluated (archetype-N/A) — no persistent data store. Cannot trigger pathway.

pathways.not_triggered_reason: INF-Q2 was Not Evaluated due to surface-gating (has_persistent_data_store=false). No database infrastructure exists to migrate.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4 Not Evaluated (archetype-N/A for stateless-utility); no data processing workloads requiring managed analytics

triggering_questions.note: Not Evaluated (archetype-N/A). No streaming/ETL/analytics artifacts found.

pathways.not_triggered_reason: INF-Q4 Not Evaluated for stateless-utility archetype. No evidence of data processing workloads that would benefit from managed analytics.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=2 (partial IaC) AND OPS-Q5=2 (basic deployment without canary)

triggering_questions.note: Only CI/CD pipeline definitions as code; no infrastructure provisioning IaC
Two-stage PyPI publish but no canary or gradual rollout

immediate_actions: Implement IaC for any supporting infrastructure

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent frameworks detected; context contains 'ML' signal term indicating AI/ML tooling intent

triggering_questions.note: Discovery-based trigger: No AI/agent frameworks (LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, Bedrock SDK) detected in dependencies; repository context tags include 'ml' indicating AI/ML tooling intent

immediate_actions: Evaluate Bedrock integration for intelligent experiment recommendations
